title: practical english usage - Part 47
content:
achievement.

The  present perfect is often used to express the idea of completion or All the wars in history have taught us nothing. We know nothing.

Have you read the Bible? -> Do you know the Bible?

Our  dog has died. -> Our  dog is dead.

Emily has had a baby. -» Emily now has a baby.

Utopia has invaded Fantasia. Utopia is at war with Fantasia.

Some fool has let the cat in. -» The cat is in.

I've broken my leg. -> My leg is broken now.

expressing the same facts.

A  present-perfect sentence often corresponds to a present-tense sentence I can't go skiing because I have broken my leg.

example if we  are giving news.

How often have you been in love in your life?

Adverbs of frequency like often, sometimes, occasionally are common with the I've written six emails since lunchtime.

times up to the present.

We   can use the present perfect to say that something has happened several 5 repetition       up   to  now:   I've  written        six    emails       . ..

I'm sure we've met before.   Has Dan phoned yet?

Have you ever seen a ghost? She's never said 'sorry' in her life.
